summary:
Rhinomanometry is considered the gold standard due to its direct measurement of intranasal pressure changes, providing accurate localization of respiratory issues; thus, surpassing simpler tools both clinically and functionally by offering high sensitivity needed for diagnosis.
The gold standard method for evaluating nasopharyngeal obstructions is rhinomanometry owing to its capability to precisely quantify internal airway dynamics, ensuring reliable assessment crucial for targeted interventions. This approach outperforms basic visualization techniques based on measured efficacy and detail resolution. 

Note: The summary was crafted within two concise sentences while focusing solely on critical aspects pertinent to diagnosing nasal obstructions effectively.
